metadata.source: consolidated from realmshelps.net and existing data

data.name: Aligned Devotion
data.prerequisite: Ability to spontaneously cast cure spells.
data.description: Your healing powers are only effective for those whose alignment closely matches yours.
data.effect: Your spells of the healing subschool have reduced effect on those who do not share your alignment. Those that have exactly the same alignment as you gain full benefit from your healing. Those with an alignment within one step of yours are cured half the normal amount of hit points from your healing spells. Characters with alignments more than one step different from yours gain no benefit from your healing at all. Your cure spells still deal full damage to undead.

data.name: Arcane Conundrum
data.description: You are unable to use your inborn magical abilities.
data.effect: You may never use any spell-like abilities under any circumstances. In addition, you suffer a -2 penalty on saving throws versus spells of the illusion school.

data.name: Arcane Fatigue
data.description: Conjuring magical energies is especially draining for you.
data.effect: Whenever you successfully cast an arcane spell you must immediately make a Fortitude save (DC 10 + spell level). If you fail you become fatigued. If you are fatigued as a result of this flaw, it has no further effect until that condition is removed.

data.name: Arcane Parasites
data.description: Thousands of unseen parasites within your flesh draw upon the arcane power held within every spell you cast.
data.effect: Every arcane spell you cast has the DC to resist it lowered by 1. These parasites cannot be removed by any mortal means, even by a miracle or wish.

data.name: Arcane Performer
data.prerequisite: Ability to cast arcane spells without preparation, Perform 1 rank.
data.description: You depend upon your performance ability to aid in the casting of spells.
data.effect: In order to cast any spell, you must succeed at a Perform check (DC 10 + spell level). If you fail, you lose the spell.

data.name: Beady Eyes
data.description: You have narrow beady eyes that do not catch the light as well as they should.
data.effect: You do not have darkvision; instead you possess low-light vision. In addition, you suffer a -2 penalty on all Search and Spot checks.

data.name: Beastly
data.description: You have more in common with beasts than you do with your own race.
data.effect: You are vulnerable to the following list of spells that affect animals, as well as those which affect your own creature type: animal trance, calm animals, charm animal, dominate animal, hide from animals, hold animal. Additionally, you suffer a -2 penalty on all Charisma-based skill checks except Disguise, Handle Animal, and Use Magic Device. Anyone observing your mannerisms who possesses the wild empathy class ability can recognize this flaw immediately.

data.name: Bestial Instinct
data.description: You prefer to meet your foes so close that you can smell their blood.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty to hit while using anything other than unarmed strikes or natural weapons.

data.name: Blind Rage
data.description: Your ability to distinguish between friend and foe is limited once you enter a rage.
data.effect: You may not end your rage voluntarily. Once you enter a rage, you continue to rage for the entire duration. While in a rage, you must attack a creature every round if able. You can charge or move in order to make the attack, and you may move to attack a foe rather than stay still to attack a friend. If there are no creatures within sight, you attack the nearest breakable object. You may attempt a DC 15 Will save once per round on your turn in order stop attacking an ally. If foes become visible, you may immediately switch targets and engage them.

data.name: Brash
data.description: You move about without careful consideration of foes.
data.effect: You suffer a -6 penalty to your Armor Class against attacks of opportunity.

data.name: Bravado
data.description: You are rash in combat, refusing to show weakness by falling back from your enemies' blows.
data.effect: You may not gain the benefit of any kind of dodge bonuses to your Armor Class. Spells, items, and effects that provide a dodge bonus provide no benefit for you. You cannot use the withdraw action during combat.

data.name: Cautious (Flaw)
data.description: You are uneasy engaging in behavior that carries a chance of failure.
data.effect: You must spend twice as long performing any skill that requires an action. For example, deciphering a page of text would take 2 minutes for you instead of 1, while the Concentration skill is not affected by this flaw as it does not require an action. You also suffer a -2 penalty on initiative checks.

data.name: Chivalrous Courtesy
data.description: You despise raising your hand against creatures of the opposite gender.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on attack rolls to hit a creature you can tell is of the opposite gender.

data.name: City Slicker (flaw)
data.description: Although interested in the natural world, you suffer from a lack of experience in the wilds.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on all Handle Animal, Knowledge (nature), and Survival checks.

data.name: Claustrophobia
data.description: You are uncomfortable in enclosed spaces.
data.effect: You become shaken while in any space where you are within 10 feet of a wall or other solid surface on 2 or more sides, or if the ceiling is less than 10 feet above your head. (Such as while walking down a 20-foot-wide corridor or while you are in any room with a 10-foot-high ceiling.) Spells and effects that remove fear do not remove this condition.

data.name: Code of Arms
data.description: Trained to only kill other armed warriors, you hesitate when attacking unarmed opponents.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on attack rolls made against an enemy not armed with a melee weapon. If the enemy uses a natural attack against you, you may then attack it without penalty.

data.name: Cold-Blooded
data.description: You were raised in the arctic and cannot tolerate heat.
data.effect: You automatically fail all Fortitude saves made to overcome the effects of high temperatures. Fire-based effects deal an additional 2 points of fire damage to you.

data.name: Coward
data.description: In dangerous circumstances, you are likely to run away.
data.effect: You automatically fail all saves against fear effects. An ability that makes you immune to fear (such as becoming a 3rd-level paladin) instead grants you a saving throw, but at a -4 penalty on the save.

data.name: Curious
data.description: You are easily distracted by the sights and sounds around you.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty on Listen and Spot checks. You also suffer a -2 penalty on initiative rolls.

data.name: Divine Gesture
data.description: Your religious rites require elaborate gestures that cause you difficulty when casting divine spells while wearing armor or using a shield.
data.effect: You suffer a spell failure chance (due to armor, shields, or anything else which causes arcane spell failure) when casting divine spells, just as if they were arcane spells.

data.name: Domain Devotion
data.description: You are limited to accessing only one of your deity's domains.
data.effect: You choose and have access to only one of your deity's domains. You may gain prestige domains from a prestige class as usual.

data.name: Elven Pride of Arms
data.description: You only consider those weapons designed specifically for use by the noblest of elves to be fit to wield. Using any other object as a weapon is beneath your dignity.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on attack rolls when using a weapon other than a longsword, rapier, or bow (long, short, or composite). Attacks made while fighting unarmed, touch attacks (including ranged touch attacks), or attacks made with natural weaponry also incur this penalty.

data.name: Exhausting Rage
data.description: You rage so powerfully that you become exhausted once you calm down.
data.effect: When you end your rage, you become exhausted (instead of fatigued) for the duration of the encounter. If you gain the ability to rage tirelessly, as a 17th-level barbarian, you are instead fatigued after a rage.

data.name: Feeble
data.description: You are unathletic and uncoordinated.
data.effect: You take a -2 penalty on Strength-, Dexterity-, and Constitution-based ability checks and skill checks.

data.name: Foe Specialist (flaw)
data.description: Your training is so specialized that you have difficulty tracking, predicting, or otherwise interacting with those foes you have not studied in great detail.
data.effect: You suffer a -1 penalty on attack rolls, Bluff, Sense Motive, Spot, and Survival checks made against creatures of any kind that isn't one of your favored enemies.

data.name: Fool
data.description: Your excessively lighthearted nature grates on your companions.
data.effect: You lose the inspire courage, inspire competence, inspire greatness, and inspire heroics bardic music abilities.

data.name: Forlorn
data.description: You lack the ability to summon a creature to do your bidding.
data.effect: You lose the ability to call a familiar.

data.name: Forlorn of Men
data.description: You are uncomfortable in the presence of humanoids.
data.effect: You become shaken if there is more than one humanoid within 30 feet of you, including members of your adventuring party or other friends. Spells and effects that remove fear do not remove this condition.

data.name: Frail
data.description: You are thin and weak of frame.
data.effect: Subtract 1 from the number of hit points you gain at each level. This flaw can reduce the number of hit points you gain to 0 (but not below).
data.special: You must have a Constitution of 4 or higher to take this flaw.

data.name: Frail (Flaw)
data.description: You become fatigued when your body is assaulted by poison or magic.
data.effect: Whenever you fail a Fortitude save, you become fatigued, in addition to any other effects you might suffer. This condition lasts until you get 8 hours of complete rest. This flaw applies only once to any given source that requires a Fortitude save. Failing a Fortitude save while fatigued causes you to become exhausted.

data.name: Free-Spirited
data.description: You seek to live your life freely, unburdened by excessive material possessions.
data.effect: The weight of any gear you carry is considered double for the purposes of determining your load. For example, a half-elf with a Strength of 10 and 30 pounds of gear is treated as carrying 60 pounds, which is a medium load. In addition, any armor check penalties you have are also doubled (or tripled, in the case of making a Swim check).

data.name: Frivolous Performer
data.description: Having focused your time on practicing and performing, you have gathered little useful information in your travels.
data.effect: You suffer a -10 penalty on all bardic knowledge checks. This penalty does not apply if the relevant knowledge involves the Perform skill in which you have the most ranks. For example, if you have the most ranks in Perform (wind instrument), you may use bardic knowledge without penalty to identify famous wind instruments, to remember facts about famous wind instrument players, or to identify places that make quality wind instruments.

data.name: Fussy
data.description: You are uncomfortable ingesting anything but a small range of preferred foods and drinks.
data.effect: You become sickened (no saving throw allowed) upon ingestion of any sort of potion for the duration of its effect. Potions with an instantaneous duration cause you to be sickened for a period of time equal to the potion's caster level in minutes. You also suffer a -4 penalty on saving throws made against ingested poisons.

data.name: Glory-Hound
data.description: You have an insatiable desire for glorious combat, fighting recklessly to prove yourself.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty to AC during combat until you drop an opponent. This penalty does not apply if the combat is against a single opponent.

data.name: Grudge Keeper
data.description: You have an overpowering need for vengeance and have difficulty letting go of grudges.
data.effect: If you are damaged in combat, you suffer a -2 penalty on attacks rolls, skill checks, saving throws, and ability checks until you damage the foe who caused you harm. This penalty does not apply if you cannot discern the source of the damage. This penalty disappears when the combat ends.

data.name: Gullible
data.description: You believe what you see, and you trust others more than you should.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty on saving throws made to resist enchantment and illusion spells and effects. You also suffer a -4 penalty on Sense Motive checks.

data.name: Half-Blood Outcast
data.description: You despise your nonhuman side.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty on all attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks when within line of sight of an elf (if you are a half-elf) or an orc if you are a half-orc).

data.name: Hatred
data.description: You are engulfed by a deep hatred of your chosen foes and you become insensibly enraged in their presence.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty to your Armor Class and on attack rolls and skill checks whenever you are not engaged in melee combat with a favored enemy and a favored enemy is close enough that you could engage it.

data.name: Haunted
data.description: You are unable to prevent the occurrence of strange noises in your immediate vicinity.
data.effect: Spontaneous noises sometimes arise in your immediate vicinity. These noises are completely out of your control and cannot be used to communicate or distract other creatures. These sounds cease when you are unconscious, petrified, asleep, or dead. You suffer a -4 penalty on all Listen and Move Silently skill checks.

data.name: Honor of The Duel
data.description: You strongly believe in one-on-one combat to decide a fight.
data.effect: You must make a Will save (DC 10 + your level) to attack a creature in a square threatened by one of its other foes. A successful save allows you to attack such a creature, albeit with a -2 penalty on your attack rolls. A failed save prohibits you from attacking that creature.

data.name: Honorable Challenge
data.description: You only cross arms with foes who willingly engage you.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on attack rolls against creatures that have not explicitly challenged you or made an attack against you. For the purposes of this feat, an attack against you includes any action that would end an invisibility spell.

data.name: Hot-Blooded
data.description: You were raised in the desert and cannot tolerate cold.
data.effect: You automatically fail all Fortitude saves made to overcome the effects of low temperatures. Cold-based effects deal an additional 2 points of cold damage to you.

data.name: Implacable
data.description: You refuse to retreat from even the most deadly of confrontations.
data.effect: If you move out of melee combat for any reason, you suffer a -2 penalty on all attack rolls, skill checks, and saving throws for 1 hour.

data.name: Inattentive (Flaw)
data.description: You are particularly unaware of your surroundings.
data.effect: You take a -4 penalty on listen checks and Spot checks.

data.name: Individualist
data.description: You are committed to a path that requires complete independence from others.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty on all attack rolls made using weapons that you have not personally crafted. In addition, the armor check penalty of any suit of armor you wear that you did not craft increases by +1.

data.name: Insomniac
data.description: You have great difficulty achieving a full and restful sleep.
data.effect: In order to get a full night of rest you must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save. If you are sleeping and a disturbance wakes you during the night, you must make an additional save (at the same DC) to return to sleep. If you fail the Fortitude save you are fatigued when you rise in the morning. If you are fatigued when you try to sleep, you gain a +2 bonus on your save. If you are exhausted, you gain a +6 bonus. Comfortable conditions, such as those found in most inns, grant you a +4 bonus on this save. Poor conditions, such as sleeping on the ground or in armor, give you a -4 penalty on this save. Even if you sleep poorly, you may prepare arcane spells as normal after 8 hours of fitful rest.

data.name: Light Sensitivity
data.description: Accustomed only to the darker places in the world, your eyes open much wider than normal, making it difficult for you to see in bright light.
data.effect: You become dazzled in bright sunlight or within the radius of a daylight spell. A dazzled creature takes a -1 penalty on attack rolls, Search checks, and Spot checks.

data.name: Lightweight
data.description: You are light for your size. Region: Constitution 13 or lower, you may not be Obese.
data.effect: You have half the base weight of a normal character of your race. Height and weight modifiers are added normally. You suffer a -4 penalty when making or resisting bull rushes, grapples, overruns, or trip attacks. You gain no attack bonus when you charge, but you do suffer the -2 Armor Class penalty and take double damage from weapons set against charges you make.

data.name: Living Faith
data.prerequisite: Ability to turn or rebuke undead.
data.description: Your religious practices give you no special powers over the dead.
data.effect: You lose the ability to turn or rebuke undead. This does not extend to any other creatures a domain might grant you the ability to turn or rebuke, such as earth elementals for a cleric with the Air domain.

data.name: Loner
data.prerequisite: Ability to summon a familiar or animal companion.
data.description: You do not have the ability to summon a familiar or gain an animal companion.
data.effect: You lose the ability to summon a familiar or gain an animal companion. You never gain the ability to gain a special companion, whether through animal companion, summon familiar, or some other class ability or feat. Possessing multiple classes that would grant you an animal companion or familiar does not negate the effects of this flaw.

data.name: Loudmouth
data.description: You are a loud, obnoxious, boisterous, and compulsive talker.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on all Diplomacy and Move Silently checks.

data.name: Love of Nature
data.description: You do not wish harm upon any natural creature.
data.effect: You must succeed at a DC 12 Will save before you can attack any creature with the animal, plant, or vermin type, or you lose your action. Note that animals, plants, and vermin may attack you without penalty.

data.name: Magical Fascination
data.description: You have a tendency to become fascinated by spells and elaborate magical effects.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty on attack rolls and skill checks when in sight of a visible magical effect with a duration greater than instantaneous (such as a

data.name: Magical Overload
data.description: You have difficulty controlling your most powerful magic, being more comfortable with more familiar, lower-level spells.
data.effect: You must use one spell slot of your highest spell level to cast or prepare a spell of a lower level. This spell slot may be used to cast or prepare any spell of any level lower than your highest level, effectively causing you to lose one of your highest-level spell slots to gain one of a lower level. For example, a 6th-level sorcerer would lose the ability to cast one 3rd-level spell per day, but would gain the ability to cast one additional 0, 1st-, or 2nd-level spell instead. This flaw always affects your highest spell level and changes the spell level it affects as you gain levels.

data.name: Material Devotion
data.description: You are restricted to using magical objects created with your deity's domains in mind.
data.effect: You may only use spell-trigger items (wands and staffs) that cast spells on your domain spell lists. Other spell-trigger items fail in your hands.

data.name: Meager Fortitude
data.description: You are sickly and weak of stomach.
data.effect: You take a -3 penalty on Fortitude saves.

data.name: Metal Intolerance
data.description: You are allergic to metals.
data.effect: You suffer an additional point of damage when struck by metal weapons. The natural attacks of inevitables, iron golems, and other creatures composed of metal also deal this additional damage.

data.name: Methodical Magical Methods
data.description: Your spellcasting methods require a rigid posture that makes you vulnerable to your foes while casting.
data.effect: You are considered flat-footed with an effective Dexterity of 0 (-5 to AC) on any round in which you cast a spell. You may not cast spells defensively.

data.name: Meticulous Performer
data.description: You get lost in your performances.
data.effect: Initiating or maintaining any bardic music ability is a full-round action.

data.name: Mounted Warrior
data.description: You only feel comfortable fighting from the saddle.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty on attack rolls made while not riding a mount.

data.name: Murky-Eyed
data.description: Your vision is obscured.
data.effect: In combat, every time you attack an opponent that has concealment, roll your miss chance twice. If either or both results indicate that you miss, your attack fails.

data.name: No Time For Book Learning
data.description: You put no faith in the written word.
data.effect: You are illiterate. You can never learn to read any language, and you suffer a -2 penalty on all Knowledge checks except Knowledge (nature).

data.name: Noncombatant
data.description: You are relatively inept at melee combat.
data.effect: You take a -2 penalty on all melee attack rolls.

data.name: Obese
data.prerequisite: Small size, Dexterity 13 or lower, you may not be Lightweight.
data.description: Your great bulk can sometimes impede your actions.
data.effect: You have double the base weight of a character of your race. Height and weight modifiers are added normally. You lose your +1 bonus to Armor Class and on attack rolls, as well as your +4 bonus on Hide checks. You must pay double the cost for armor but not for any magical enhancements the armor might have. Such armor does not weigh half the normal amount.

data.name: Pathetic
data.description: You are weaker in an attribute than you should be.
data.effect: Reduce one of your ability scores by 2.
data.special: You cannot take this flaw if the total of your ability modifiers is 8 or higher.

data.name: Phantom Spark
data.description: Your body occasionally emits bursts of bright colored light.
data.effect: You spontaneously emit bursts of colored light. These lights are completely out of your control and cannot be used as a light source. They only cease when you are unconscious, petrified, asleep, or dead. You suffer a -4 penalty on all Hide and Spot skill checks.

data.name: Ponderous Spellcaster
data.description: Your spells take additional time to cast.
data.effect: The casting time of all your spells are doubled. Spells that normally require a single action to cast require a full-round action to cast instead. Spells that normally require a free action to cast now require a standard action to cast instead.

data.name: Poor Reflexes
data.description: You often zig when you should have zagged.
data.effect: You take a -3 penalty on Reflex saves.

data.name: Pride of Arms
data.description: You take great pride in knowing how to use weapons specifically designed for war. You consider all other weapons beneath your station and have allowed your training in them to atrophy.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on all attack rolls made with exotic weapons, simple weapons, unarmed attacks, and touch attacks.

data.name: Quarter Elf
data.description: Elven blood is not particularly strong in your veins and its features are barely noticeable.
data.effect: You are not immune to sleep spells and do not gain a racial bonus on saving throws against enchantment spells or effects. You are not considered to have elven blood.

data.name: Quick Burning Rage
data.description: Your rages don't last very long.
data.effect: Your rage only lasts for a number of rounds equal to your newly improved Constitution modifier.

data.name: Restricted Sorcery
data.prerequisite: Ability to cast spells without preparation.
data.description: Your magical powers manifest within a limited realm of effects.
data.effect: Choose two schools of magic to be your restricted schools. When you select spells to learn, you may not choose spells from these two restricted schools. In addition, you may not use wands or scrolls that cast spells from these restricted schools. You may not choose divination as one of your restricted schools.

data.name: Shaky
data.description: You are relatively poor at ranged combat.
data.effect: You take a -2 penalty on all ranged attack rolls.

data.name: Short Attention Span
data.description: You have little patience, causing you to have difficulty concentrating on repetitive tasks.
data.effect: You suffer a -2 penalty on all skill checks made to retry an action that you previously failed. This penalty is cumulative, increasing by -2 for every failed attempt. In addition, you may not take 20 on any skill check.

data.name: Short of Breath
data.description: You have limited stamina and you easily become exhausted by physical tasks.
data.effect: Whenever you make a Strength check, a Constitution check to continue running, or use the skills Climb, Jump, or Swim, you must make a Fortitude save at the same DC as the skill or ability check. If you fail this save, you become fatigued.

data.name: Short Temper
data.description: You become angry and flustered under stressful circumstances.
data.effect: If you suffer any damage during combat, you must make a DC 15 Will save or become enraged. While enraged you suffer a -2 penalty to your Armor Class and you cannot use any Charisma-, Dexterity-, or Intelligence-based skills (except for Balance, Escape Artist, Intimidate, and Ride), the Concentration skill, or any abilities that require patience or concentration. You cannot cast spells or activate magic items that require a command word, a spell trigger (such as a wand), or spell completion (such as a scroll) to function. You can use any feat you possess except Combat Expertise, item creation feats, and metamagic feats. This enraged state lasts for a number of rounds equal to 3 + your Constitution modifier and cannot be ended prematurely. This penalty stacks with those from the use of the barbarian rage ability, resulting in a -4 AC penalty.

data.name: Skulker
data.description: You are not prepared to stand face to face with your foes.
data.effect: You must make a DC 15 Will save or become shaken whenever a foe engages you in melee. This penalty takes effect as soon as an enemy attacks you while you are within its threatened area. You recover from your fear as soon as you are not within an area threatened by foes.

data.name: Slow (Flaw)
data.description: You move exceptionally slowly.
data.effect: Your base land speed is halved (round down to the nearest 5-foot interval).
data.special: You must have a base land speed of at least 20 feet to take this flaw.

data.name: Slow Healing
data.description: You do not heal quickly.
data.effect: You do not normally recover hit points or temporary ability damage through natural healing. If you undergo complete bed rest for a full day, you may recover either 1 hit point or 1 point of temporary ability damage. In addition, any conjuration (healing) spell cast upon you works at only half its caster level. For example, a cure moderate wounds cast upon you by an 8th-level cleric would only heal 2d8+4 points of damage, instead of 2d8+8 points of damage.

data.name: Slow To Anger
data.description: It takes you longer to get worked up.
data.effect: Entering a rage requires a full- round action

data.name: Solitary Paragon
data.description: You prefer to fight alone.
data.effect: You gain no benefit from flanking a foe and instead suffer a -4 penalty on attack rolls made against a foe you flank.

data.name: Stubby Fingers
data.description: You have short stubby fingers, which are not well-suited for grasping.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on all attack rolls made when you use a light or one-handed weapon (natural weapons and unarmed attacks do not incur this penalty). In addition, you suffer a -4 penalty on all Disable Device, Open Lock, Sleight of Hand, and Use Rope checks.

data.name: Superstitious
data.description: You fear magic, and avoid it when possible.
data.effect: Whenever you see an item, effect, or location obviously created by magic (whether by spell or some other means), you become frightened. The frightened condition lasts until you can no longer see the obviously magical item, effect, or location.

data.name: Terrain Specialist
data.description: You were trained to use your abilities in only one type of environment and are uncomfortable and less competent in all others.
data.effect: Choose one terrain type (aquatic, desert, forest, hills, marsh, mountain, or plains). That is your home terrain, in which you are most comfortable. In all other terrains you suffer a -2 penalty on all wild empathy checks, as well as on Search or Survival checks made to track. You may only use your camouflage and hide in plain sight abilities in your chosen terrain.

data.name: Trivial Performer
data.description: Your performance is lighthearted and has limited effect on others.
data.effect: The save DC against your fascinate, suggestion, and mass suggestion abilities is half normal (rounded down). If you use the inspire courage, inspire greatness, or inspire heroics bardic music abilities, their benefits end as soon as you stop performing (rather than lasting for an additional 5 rounds).

data.name: Uncontrollable Rage
data.description: You have no ability to control your rage.
data.effect: You cannot enter a rage voluntarily. If during the previous round something dealt you damage, grappled or immobilized you, or you failed a saving throw against a spell, you may enter a rage; if you do not wish to enter a rage after such an event, you must succeed at a DC 15 Will save or enter the rage involuntarily. You cannot enter a rage more times per day than you are normally entitled to.

data.name: Unreactive
data.description: You are slow to react to danger.
data.effect: You take a -6 penalty on initiative checks.

data.name: Vulnerable
data.description: You are not good at defending yourself.
data.effect: You take a -1 penalty to Armor Class.

data.name: Warrior of The Phalanx
data.description: Trained to fight in a group, you have difficulties when fighting alone.
data.effect: You suffer a -4 penalty on attack rolls you make when not adjacent to an ally.

data.name: Weak Will
data.description: You are highly suggestible and easily duped.
data.effect: You take a -3 penalty on Will saves.

data.name: Weapon Bound
data.description: You are attuned to your deity's weapon and no other.
data.effect: You lose proficiency with all weapons except the favored weapon of your deity. This flaw does not grant you proficiency with that weapon.

data.name: Wild
data.description: Accustomed only to a free life in the wild, you panic when captured or restrained.
data.effect: Whenever you are entangled, pinned, or bound, you immediately become panicked. Your fear is alleviated 1 round after you are free of all bonds. Spells and effects that remove fear do not prevent this effect.
